vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

risk.desc: Potential for thermal stress due to high heat generation from the harmonic drive
risk.mit: Implement thermal management features such as heat sinks and cooling channels

risk.desc: Risk of cable harness damage due to insufficient strain relief
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief geometry and adequate bend radius

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under high torque loads
risk.mit: Conduct FEA analysis to validate structural integrity and reinforce if necessary

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to unclear assembly sequence
risk.mit: Define a clear assembly sequence with detailed steps and tools

risk.desc: Risk of improper fit due to insufficient tolerance stack-up analysis
risk.mit: Conduct tolerance stack-up analysis and ensure fit_class is defined for all interfaces

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409
physics_paths: load: Base plate carries the entire arm's weight and torque from the base to the end effector
flow: Electrical cables and data harnesses pass through the base plate
thermal: Heat from the motor and harmonic drive is dissipated through the base plate
motion: Base joint rotates around the Z-axis, allowing the arm to move in a circular motion
pressure: Seals prevent ingress of dust and water, maintaining IP54 rating
human/service access: Leveling feet and cable entry grommets provide access for maintenance and service
integration_opportunities: Base plate can be integrated with the floor mounting flange
Harmonic drive can be integrated with the motor housing
Cable harness can be integrated with the control system

manufacturing_strategy.process: CNC machining + LPBF (Laser Powder Bed Fusion)
manufacturing_strategy.support: Orientation: Base plate horizontal, harmonic drive vertical
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and CMM inspection for critical dimensions

verification_focus: Verify the base plate's structural integrity under 100Nm torque
Verify the harmonic drive's alignment and fit
Verify the cable harness's strain relief and routing

Pneumatic lines maintain pressure for joint actuation.
Cable spine and harness channels are externally accessible for maintenance and inspection.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic
physics_paths: Load path: Cable spine from base to wrist transfers payload and joint torque; strain relief at each joint prevents damage.
Flow path: Signal and power cables routed through harness channels; pneumatic lines through dedicated channels.
Thermal path: Cable spine and harness channels dissipate heat from electronics and motor.
Motion path: Slack loops at each joint allow arm motion without cable strain.
Pressure path: Pneumatic lines maintain pressure for joint actuation.
Human/service access path: Cable spine and harness channels are externally accessible for maintenance and inspection.
integration_opportunities: Cable spine design allows for easy replacement of signal and power cables.
Harness channels are accessible for maintenance and inspection.
Slack loops provide sufficient cable length for full arm motion range.
Cable spine and harness channels are protected from mechanical damage.
Connector accessibility ensures connectors can be mated and unmated without disassembly.
verification_focus: Load path closure: Ensure cable spine and harness channels can support 5kg payload and 50Nm joint torque.
Strain relief: Verify strain relief geometry prevents cable damage during arm motion.
Cable spine accessibility: Confirm cable spine and harness channels are externally accessible for maintenance.
Connector accessibility: Ensure connectors are reachable for mating and unmating.
Joint clearance: Validate joint clearance allows full arm motion range without cable strain.

Power and signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles

PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts

Interfaces include fit_class and stack_up_mm
Assembly order is defined with tool, torque, datum per step
Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, drill, subtract, pocket
Dimensions match robot_arm scale: 80-400mm per link, 600-1200mm reach
Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible

verify.rule: Geometry_ops args.at [x,y,z] must be physically distributed across the local subsystem volume.

verify.value: cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
verify.rule: cad_brief must include assembly_sequence (ordered list of {step:int, action:install|fasten|align|press|route, target:string, tool:string, torque_Nm_or_force_N:number, datum:string})

verify.value: Boundary_conditions object is present with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.
verify.rule: verify must include a boundary_conditions object with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.

risk.desc: Potential thermal management issues due to high power density in the driver bay.
risk.mit: Implement active cooling solutions such as heat sinks or liquid cooling.

risk.desc: Risk of signal interference due to proximity of power and signal channels.
risk.mit: Ensure sufficient separation between power and signal channels or implement shielding.

risk.desc: Risk of improper grounding leading to electrical noise or failure.
risk.mit: Verify grounding scheme and ensure signal returns route alongside their signals.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for PCBs leading to mechanical interference.
risk.mit: Ensure PCBs are mounted on standoffs with adequate clearance from walls and moving parts.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly errors due to ambiguous build sequence.
risk.mit: Ensure clear and ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Drive electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window
physics_paths: power_channel: from motor_driver_board to motor
signal_channel: from encoder_board to controller_board
thermal_inspection_window: allows IR camera to inspect internal components
ground_point: chassis ground connection for electronics modules
service_cover: provides access for maintenance and diagnostics
integration_opportunities: driver_bay_shell can accommodate additional cooling fins
signal_channel can be routed through a shielded conduit
thermal_inspection_window can be placed on the opposite side for better visibility

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: visual and CMM for critical dimensions
